specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a personal engineering blog for long-form essays. It should feel editorial rather than startup-ish, and the page must visibly include a featured essay, reading time labels, newsletter signup, quote pullout styling, recent essays, an archive view, and a quiet author note section.

Build a runnable glossary site of computer science terms for front-end developers. It must visibly include a glossary search, alphabetical navigation, opinionated definition cards, front-end developer framing, a vote or request definitions panel, and contributor notes that improvements to the website are welcome even if new definitions are not accepted directly.

Build a runnable troubleshooting flowchart for developers overwhelmed by the React ecosystem. The preview must visibly include a big flowchart or diagram area, yes/no decision nodes, advice about learning vanilla React before extra state libraries, bundler confusion guidance, and an Open in new tab action for the chart.

Build the first runnable preview of a tool for exploring the React Server Components protocol. It must visibly include a streamed row inspector, server and client panes, a React tree view, step-by-step stream playback, example presets like hello, async, counter, form action, and pagination, plus a short note that both server and client behavior are approximated in the browser.

Create a complete VS Code theme extension for a minimalistic, opinionated editor theme focused mostly on JavaScript. Return package.json plus a theme JSON file and a short README. It must include a hidden-status-bar minimalist philosophy, JavaScript-focused token colors, and settings suggestions like hiding line numbers, activity bar, minimap, and status bar.

Create a small React package that wraps a lag radar performance widget for development use. Return package metadata, the React component source, and a README. The package must expose props for frames, speed, size, and inset, explain that it helps detect dropped frames or responsiveness issues, and show a minimal usage example with a self-closing component.

Build the first runnable preview of a React Suspense render-as-you-fetch explorer. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a user profile card that loads inside a Suspense boundary with a visible skeleton fallback, an error boundary demo that catches and displays a failed fetch, a side-by-side fetch-on-render versus render-as-you-fetch comparison panel, a waterfall diagram showing the difference in request timing, a notes panel explaining why render-as-you-fetch eliminates sequential loading waterfalls, and a retry action.

followUps.label: add-nested-suspense
followUps.prompt: Add a nested Suspense demo where an inner boundary resolves independently from the outer one, showing both loading states simultaneously.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a Claude Skills creator and tester app. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a skill editor panel where the user writes a skill definition in markdown or YAML, a live preview panel showing the generated system prompt, a test runner that sends the skill to a mock model endpoint and streams back a response, a skill catalog sidebar with at least three example skills, a version history dropdown, and an export button that copies the skill definition.

followUps.label: add-skill-rating
followUps.prompt: Add a quality rating panel to the test runner that scores the model response on relevance, length, and tone.

specs.prompt: Build a runnable Flux architecture explorer. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include an animated data flow diagram showing Action, Dispatcher, Store, and View layers, a live action dispatch panel where users can fire named actions, a store state viewer that updates in real time, a router-integrated navigation example with at least two routes, a shopping list demo driven entirely by Flux, and a comparison panel mapping Flux concepts to modern Redux and Zustand equivalents.
